title: Bruins’ prospect James Hagens turns pro, will begin career in Providence
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens, a top prospect for the Boston Bruins, signed an AHL amateur tryout agreement after two seasons at Boston College.
Hagens will start his professional hockey career with the Providence Bruins and is expected to play his first AHL game Wednesday.
The 19-year-old forward recorded 47 points in 34 games for Boston College, helping them win their first Beanpot since 2016.

title: 7 X-factors on 2026 Red Sox roster
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The 2026 Red Sox are relying on key players like Marcelo Mayer and Brayan Bello for success.
Roman Anthony is considered an MVP candidate with expectations rising after his impressive rookie year.
Payton Tolle, a top prospect not starting the season in MLB, could impact as a bullpen option.

title: State House Minority Leader opts not to run again after 32 years in office
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Bradley H. Jones Jr. will not seek reelection after serving as Massachusetts House Minority Leader since 2002.
Jones, 61, was first sworn into office in March 1994 and has served the 20th Middlesex District for 32 years.
Massachusetts Republican Party Chair Amy Carnevale praised Jones for his dedication and impact over his long tenure.

title: No ICE agents at Logan Airport yet as agents deploy to busy airports
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: No ICE agents were deployed at Logan Airport as Trump sends federal agents to busy airports nationwide.
ICE agents could be deployed to New England airports next week, but this remains unsubstantiated, says TSA union president.
Funds for DHS, including ICE, are central to the ongoing government shutdown affecting airport security operations.

title: Fitchburg fire chief on paid leave pending external investigation, mayor says
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Fitchburg Fire Chief Dante Suarez is on paid leave pending the outcome of an external investigation.
Assistant Chief Patrick Haverty has taken over as acting chief during Suarez's absence.
Suarez's leave began on February 26, 2026, but specific details remain undisclosed by the city.

title: Person seriously injured in Seaport stabbing
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A man was seriously injured in a stabbing in Seaport, suffering wounds to his abdomen and leg.
Police responded at 2:44 a.m. Sunday near 72 Seaport Blvd, finding the victim outside 60 Seaport Blvd.
The victim was treated and hospitalized; no suspects identified, and the investigation is ongoing.

title: Here’s what happened during the first day of Kelsey Fitzsimmons’s trial
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Kelsey Fitzsimmons's trial commenced in Essex County Superior Court, where she waived her right to a jury trial.
Fitzsimmons, a North Andover officer, is charged with assault and battery; claims she attempted suicide when shot by a colleague.
Prosecutors allege Fitzsimmons aimed a weapon at Officer Noonan, who fired in defense; the defense argues it's a cover-up.

title: Bruins host Blackstone Valley co-op hockey team at practice after run to R.I. state title ​
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins hosted the Blackstone Valley co-op team at Warrior Ice Arena after their state title win.
Blackstone Valley celebrated their Rhode Island Division II championship with the Bruins, following personal tragedies.
Bruins' Jeremy Swayman and coach Marco Sturm engaged with the team, emphasizing hockey's close-knit community.

title: Patriots sign offensive tackle James Hudson
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The New England Patriots signed offensive tackle James Hudson, recently released by the Giants, to add depth to their roster.
Hudson has played 60 games with 19 starts, mainly at right tackle; he offers experience backing up current starter Morgan Moses.
Hudson previously played for the Cleveland Browns and New York Giants, bringing experience from his five NFL seasons.

title: As waiting game continues with James Hagens, Bruins coach has no issue adding prospects to lineup
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens has not yet signed a professional contract with the Bruins following his sophomore season at Boston College.
Hagens recorded 47 points in 34 games, helping Boston College win its first Beanpot title since 2016.
Bruins coach Marco Sturm is open to adding prospects to the lineup, following their performance readiness.
